title: Expansible intervertebral implant
abstract: The present invention relates to an intervertebral implant comprising : â¢ - a baseplate (6) at the proximal end, â¢ - a plurality of elongated arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) along the primary axis (X) and each including at least one vertebral support surface (4a, 4b, 4c, 4d), the arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) being articulated at least on the baseplate (6) such that the implant comprises on the one hand a folded-back position in which the arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) are close to each other, and on the other hand a deployed position in which the arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) are moved away from each other along at least one secondary axis (Y) and/or at least one tertiary axis (Z), the primary (X), secondary (Y) and tertiary (Z) axes being substantially orthogonal to each other; â¢ -expansion means (3, 8, 21, 21 a, 21 b, 22, 25, 66, 67, 151) of the implant between the folded-back and deployed positions, comprising at least two branches (151) pivotably mounted one relative to the other and attached to the elongated arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) by at least guide means (66, 67).
claims:
Claims 1. An intervertebral implant comprising a body (20) extending longitudinally along a primary axis (X), between a proximal end and a distal end, characterized in that it includes: - a baseplate (6) at the proximal end, - a plurality of elongated arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) along the primary axis (X) and each including at least one vertebral support surface (4a, 4b, 4c, 4d), the arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) being articulated at least on the baseplate (6) such that the implant comprises on the one hand a folded-back position in which the arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) are close to each other, and on the other hand a deployed position in which the arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) are moved away from each other along at least one secondary axis (Y) and/or at least one tertiary axis (Z), the primary (X), secondary (Y) and tertiary (Z) axes being substantially orthogonal to each other; - expansion means (3, 8, 21 , 21 a, 21 b, 22, 25, 66, 67, 151) of the implant between the folded-back and deployed positions, comprising at least two branches (151) pivotably mounted one relative to the other and attached to the elongated arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) by at least guide means (66, 67) such that translation of the expansion means (3, 8, 21 , 21 a, 21 b, 22, 25, 66, 67, 151) parallel to the primary axis (X) causes pivoting of the branches (151) one relative to the other and moves the arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) away from each other, along the secondary axis (Y) and/or the tertiary axis (Z). 2. The intervertebral implant according to claim 1 , characterized in that the expansion means comprise at least one insert (3) which may be screwed through the baseplate (6) to cause translation of the expansion means (3, 8, 21 , 21a, 21 b, 22, 25, 66, 67, 151) relative to the body (20) of the implant during deployment of the elongated arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d). 3. The intervertebral implant according to one of claims 1 to 2, characterized in that pivoting of the branches (151) one relative to the other occurs around at least one pivoting axis (25) oriented parallel to the secondary axis (Y) to move away the elongated arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) along this axis and enable expansion of the width of the implant. 4. The intervertebral implant according to one of claims 1 to 3, characterized in that the expansion means of the implant also comprise expansion means in height (3, 8, 25, 66, 67, 151) of the implant, along an axis parallel to the tertiary axis (Z), using the guide means (66, 67) comprising inner surfaces (67) of the arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) which are non-parallel to each other and divergent in the direction of the distal end, such that the arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) are moved away from each other along the tertiary axis (Z) during translation of the expansion means along the primary axis (X). 5. The intervertebral implant according to one of claims 1 to 4, characterized in that pivoting of the branches (151) one relative to the other occurs around at least one pivoting axis (25) oriented parallel to the tertiary axis (Z) to move the elongated arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) away along this axis and enable expansion of the height of the implant. 6. The intervertebral implant according to one of claims 1 to 5, characterized in that the expansion means of the implant also comprise expansion means in width (22, 21 , 21a, 21 b) of the implant, along an axis parallel to the secondary axis (Y). 7. The intervertebral implant according to claim 6, characterized in that the expansion means in width (22, 21 , 21a, 21 b) of the implant comprise flexible blades (21) secured to the distal end of the arms (2a, 2b) of the implant and slidably mounted relative to the rest of the arms (2a, 2b), but connected to the expansion means in height, such that actuation of the translation causes sliding and deformation of the flexible blades, causing expansion of the width covered by the implant. 8. The intervertebral implant according to claim 6, characterized in that the expansion means in width (22, 21 , 21 a, 21 b) of the implant comprise pivot arms (21 a, 21 b) pivotably mounted on a pivoting axis (22) and with respect to the body (20) between the folded-back position in which the pivot arms (21) are substantially parallel to the pivoting axis (22) and border the body (20), and the deployed position in which the arms (21a, 21 b) are moved away from the body (20) and non-parallel to the pivoting axis (22) so as to deploy said implant along the secondary axis (Y). 9. The intervertebral implant according to one of claims 1 to 8, characterized in that the baseplate (6) comprises an articulation (64) capable of joining the arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) together on the proximal end of the body (20), the articulation (64) forming a ring segment. 10. The intervertebral implant according to one of claims 1 to 9, characterized in that the implant (1) comprises articulations (61 , 62, 63) articulating the baseplate (6) and the elongated arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d) between the folded-back position and the deployed position. 1 1. The intervertebral implant according to claim 10, characterized in that the articulations (61 , 62, 63) form reciprocal coupling means (62, 63) between the baseplate (6) and at least one of the elongated arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d). 12. The intervertebral implant according to claim 10, characterized in that the articulations (61 , 62, 63) comprise at least one plate (61) articulated at the same time on the baseplate (6) and on the proximal end of at least one elongated arm (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d). 13. The intervertebral implant according to claim 12, characterized in that the articulations (61 , 62, 63) form reciprocal coupling means (62, 63), on the one hand, between the plate (61) and the baseplate (6), and on the other hand, between the plate (61) and the proximal end of at least one of the elongated arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d). 14. The intervertebral implant according to one of claims 4 to 13, characterized in that the guide means (66, 67) comprise at least one projection (66) disposed on the expansion means, and at least one groove (67) disposed on at least one of the elongated arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d), such that the groove (67) guides the projection (66) along the body (20) during deployment of the elongated arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d). 15. The intervertebral implant according to one of claims 1 to 14, characterized in that the expansion means (3, 8, 25, 66, 67, 151) comprise at least one hooking means (8) with at least one instrumentation (PI, P3, D, D30, D31) and connected to the branches (151) so as to cause translation of the expansion means 

 (3, 25, 66, 67, 151) relative to the body (20) of the implant (1) during deployment of the elongated arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d). 16. The intervertebral implant according to one of claims 2 to 15, characterized in that the insert (3) is configured to dissociate itself into at least two fragments, forming said branches (151), to cause translation of the expansion means (3, 8, 21 , 21 a, 21 b, 22, 25, 66, 67, 151) with respect to the body (20) of the implant (1) during deployment of the elongated arms (2a, 2b, 2c, 2d). 17. The intervertebral implant according to one of claims 1 to 15, characterized in that the branches (151) are disposed on the distal end of said body (20) of the implant (1) and comprise at least one projecting portion (31) for anchoring said implant (1) on at least one vertebra (V). 18. The intervertebral implant according to one of claims 1 to 17, characterized in that the implant (1) comprises at least one anchoring device (DA) capable of passing through a passage passing through at least one portion of said implant (1) between a periphery of the implant (1) and at least one vertebra (V) and fixing said implant (1) on said vertebra (V). 19. The intervertebral implant according to claim 18, characterized in that the baseplate (6) comprises at least one guide groove adapted to at least partially guide the anchoring device (DA). 20. The intervertebral implant according to one of claims 18 and 19, characterized in that the baseplate (6) comprises at least one orifice adapted to at least partially receive an anchoring guide (GA) of the anchoring device (DA). 21. Instrumentation for the implantation of at least one intervertebral implant (1) according to one of claims 1 to 20, between at least two vertebrae (V), characterized in that it comprises at least the following instruments: - an implant holder tube (PI) capable of being fixed onto the baseplate (6) of the implant (1) and guiding it towards the intervertebral space, - a deployer (D) capable of being inserted in the implant holder tube (PI) and including a connection rod (P3) capable of enabling actuation of the expansion means. 22. The instrumentation according to claim 21 , characterized in that actuation is performed by fastening the connection rod (P3) onto the hooking means (8) of the implant (1). 23. The instrumentation according to one of claims 21 and 22, characterized in that it comprises at least one charger (CA, CG) capable of receiving at least one anchoring device (DA) and/or a graft (G), and at least one impacter (IG) capable of impacting anchoring devices (DA) or delivering a graft (G) through the charger (CG). 24. The instrumentation according to one of claims 21 to 23, characterized in that it further comprises an implantation pistol (P) including a handle (PPI) capable of connecting with the implant holder tube (PI) via connection means (CPI) so as to implant at least one implant (1), an anchoring device (DA) and/or a graft (G) towards the vertebra (V). 25. The instrumentation according to claim 24, characterized in that the instruments, such as the deployer (D), the charger (CA, CG) or the impacter (IG), may be coupled and uncoupled successively onto and from the pistol (P) during implantation. 26. The instrumentation according to one of claims 24 and 25, characterized in that the pistol may be connected and disconnected with or without the presence of the instruments, such as the deployer, the charger or the impacter, in the tube of the implant holder.
